evals.prompt: Generate a cold email to Ardent Plumbing LLC in Plano, TX. 4.8 stars, 121 reviews. Website: http://ardentplumbing.com/. No SSL. CRO findings: contact form buried at bottom, cart icon present, chatbot popup. PageSpeed: 59. Owner email: micah@micahrich.com.
evals.expected_output: Should follow 5-part structure: verified compliment (4.8/121), honest positioning with case study name, one verified observation (no SSL or buried form), offer with outcome+time+guarantee, Loom CTA. Should NOT fabricate load times or UI bugs not in CRO data. Signature must be exact. 150-200 words.
assertions: Opens with verified compliment citing 4.8 stars and 121 reviews
Part 2 includes honest positioning with a named case study business
Observation traceable to real data (no SSL or CRO findings)
Offer includes outcome + time (48 hours) + guarantee (don't love it = $0)
Closes with Loom CTA (2-minute Loom walkthrough)
Signature exact: — Ola Oluwaseun / Divine Web · divineweb.co
150-200 words total
No fabricated load times or percentages
No technical jargon (PageSpeed, CRO, responsive)
Tone reads as honest peer, not corporate vendor

evals.prompt: Generate subject lines for a cold email to Jake the Plumbing Pro in Dallas. 4.8 stars, 170 reviews.
evals.expected_output: Should generate 3 subject lines in cliffhanger/internal style. Should sound like a colleague or someone who knows the business. NO Title Case, NO 'Free audit', NO marketing language.
assertions: 3 subject lines generated
All lowercase or sentence case
At least one sounds like an internal team message
None contain 'website redesign', 'free audit', or marketing language
Under 60 characters each
Creates curiosity or cliffhanger effect

evals.prompt: Generate a cold email for Premier Plumbing Solutions in Irving. 5.0 stars, 30 reviews. PageSpeed: 49. CRO: site not fully mobile responsive, quote form has phone+email+message+reCAPTCHA. No owner name. Email: premierplumbingsolution@gmail.com.
evals.expected_output: Should cite real PageSpeed score of 49 in business terms. Should reference CRO findings about the form. Should include zero-risk offer and Loom CTA. Should NOT invent additional UI bugs not in CRO data.
assertions: Cites PageSpeed 49 in business terms (not raw score)
References form complexity from CRO data
Does NOT fabricate UI bugs not in CRO findings
Includes offer with outcome+time+guarantee
Closes with Loom CTA
Honest positioning with case study name
No forbidden phrases or jargon

evals.prompt: Evaluate this lead: Service Squad Plumbing, 4.9 stars, 1384 reviews, website URL contains /locations/fort-worth-tx-plumbing/.
evals.expected_output: Should disqualify immediately. Pattern 1: review_count > 1000. Pattern 2: /locations/ in URL indicates franchise. Should NOT pass to scoring.
assertions: Identifies review_count > 1000 as hard disqualifier
Identifies /locations/ URL pattern as franchise indicator
Returns status disqualified
Does NOT assign a high ICP fit score
Cites specific doe-learnings pattern numbers

evals.prompt: Generate a cold email for a lead with pagespeed_mobile: NULL, load_time_ms: NULL. Business: Tom's Plumbing Inc, Arlington TX, 4.4 stars, 85 reviews. CRO: has_contact_form=true, has_cta=true, has_click_to_call=false, design_score=4.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT cite any specific load time or seconds value since pagespeed is NULL. Should use generic language about mobile experience. Should reference CRO finding of no click-to-call. Should include full 5-part structure.
assertions: Does NOT cite any specific load time or seconds
Uses generic mobile language if mentioning speed at all
References no click-to-call from CRO data
Includes verified compliment with 4.4/85
Includes offer with outcome+time+guarantee
Closes with Loom CTA
Signature exact
